name: Redondo and Castelo de Valongo Loop
description: This moderate 37.5-mile (60.4 km) road cycling loop from Redondo, Portugal, features historic castles and 1316 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Redondo offers a network of routes through the region's varied landscape. The area features rolling countryside with moderate elevation changes, suitable for road cyclists seeking both endurance challenges and scenic rides. Many routes are loops, providing diverse perspectives of the local terrain and historical points of interest. The region's topography includes hills and open stretches, making it conducive to road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Redondo The most popular road cycling route is Serra d'Ossa Cycling Loop, a 49.8 miles (80.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the Serra d'Ossa region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Redondo and Castelo de Valongo Loop, a moderate 37.5 miles (60.4 km) path. This route combines countryside cycling with a visit to Castelo de Valongo, offering historical views. Local road cyclists also love the Loop from Redondo to Alandroal, a 65.5 miles (105.4 km) trail leading through the Alandroal area, often completed in about 4 hours 49 minutes. Road cycling around Redondo is defined by rolling hills, open countryside, and routes connecting historical towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer rides. The routes in Redondo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Redondo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Redondo?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Redondo, offering a variety of experiences. The region features routes suitable for different skill levels, from moderate loops to more challenging, longer rides.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Redondo?
A: While most routes around Redondo are classified as moderate, there is at least one easy route available. The region's rolling countryside generally offers moderate elevation changes, making it accessible for those with some cycling experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Redondo's road cycling routes?
A: Redondo's road cycling routes are characterized by rolling hills and open countryside. You'll find a mix of moderate elevation changes and flat stretches, connecting historical towns and offering diverse perspectives of the local terrain.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in the Redondo area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Redondo are loops, providing diverse perspectives of the local terrain. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313614297/serra-d-ossa-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313595247/redondo-and-castelo-de-valongo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondo and Castelo de Valongo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes around Redondo?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region offers more difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313608817/loop-from-redondo-to-alandroal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Redondo to Alandroal&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 65 miles with significant elevation gain.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect historical towns and feature significant landmarks. You can cycle past impressive structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/471456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Evoramonte Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7275262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7572855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vila Viçosa Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313595247/redondo-and-castelo-de-valongo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondo and Castelo de Valongo Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers historical views of Castelo de Valongo.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or unique landscapes to explore by bike?
A: Yes, the region is home to natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4266510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montado Cork Oak Forest&lt;/a&gt;, which you can explore on some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313614297/serra-d-ossa-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the scenic Serra d'Ossa region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Redondo?
A: The road cycling routes in Redondo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the connection to historical towns, and the beautiful open countryside.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through towns or villages for amenities?
A: Yes, the network of routes often connects historical towns and villages, providing opportunities to stop for amenities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gadanha Fountain, Estremoz – Alandroal town centre loop&lt;/a&gt; passes through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5149967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alandroal town centre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the wider region?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Evoramonte Castle – Estremoz loop from Aldeia da Serra&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive views of the Alentejo landscape, including distant castles and rolling plains.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Redondo?
A: The Alentejo region, where Redondo is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.
tips: Originally built on Muslim fortifications, the castle dates mainly from the 14th century, with evidence of later reconstruction phases (15th century). It is a National Monument and stands out for its defensive architecture. Although some parts are in ruins, it retains original elements such as the menagem tower (keep), arrow slits and sections of the wall, offering a glimpse into the military history of the region.

In 1250, a foral (charter) attributed to King D. Afonso III was issued to Redondo. At the same time, the king ordered the construction of a castle over the ruins of the ancient Roman fortress.

The castle was part of the northern Alentejo fortifications restructured by King D. Dinis that were donated to members of the nobility during the late medieval epoch, representing significant alterations during the Manueline era. By order of King D. Dinis, in 1312, the castle was reconstructed, during the same epoch as the erection of the keep tower and the fortification wall and towers. On 27 April 1318, the foral was reconfirmed by King D. Dinis.

In 1418, King D. John conceded privileges to the castle and obligated that travellers between Alandroal and Vila Viçosa pass through the settlement, as a way of expanding commercial activities in the town.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Loop tour from Redondo?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour from Redondo is approximately 60.4 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 35 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Loop tour from Redondo is rated as moderate. It features an elevation gain of about 401 meters, making it suitable for cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: As a racebike tour, you can expect mostly paved roads suitable for road cycling. The route includes some elevation changes, contributing to its moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by two historical castles. You'll have the opportunity to see Castelo de Valongo and the Redondo Castle.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ecopista de Reguengos and the Ramal de Reguengos.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details aren't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter rains.

faqs.question: Where does the Loop tour from Redondo start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The tour originates from Redondo. Specific parking information is not detailed, but as a loop, it typically starts and ends in the same location, suggesting parking would be available within the town of Redondo or nearby.

faqs.question: Is this racebike tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike tour primarily on roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically recommended to bring dogs on a racebike tour of this distance and difficulty for their safety and comfort, especially if they are running alongside.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate racebike tour like this?
faqs.answer: For a moderate racebike tour of this length, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A helmet is essential for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments are not listed, as the route passes through or near towns like Redondo and Valongo, you can expect to find cafes or small shops for refreshments in these areas.
